title: Mouthwatering Nutty Vietnamese Chickpeas Cha Gio
description: A nutty and indulgent Vietnamese dish featuring chickpeas, perfect for a low-carb friendly meal, formed into crispy cha gio.
instructions: In a large bowl, mash the chickpeas using a fork or a potato masher until they are mostly smooth.
Add the toasted peanuts, shredded carrots, shredded zucchini, soy sauce, grated ginger, garlic, salt, and black pepper to the bowl with the chickpeas and mix until well combined.
Fill a large bowl with warm water and have the rice paper wrappers ready to use.
Submerge a rice paper wrapper into the warm water for about 10 seconds, until it becomes soft and pliable.
Remove the rice paper wrapper from the water and place it on a clean surface, then add about 1/4 cup of the chickpea mixture to the center of the wrapper.
Fold the bottom half of the wrapper over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder, repeating with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it reaches 350 degrees.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the cha gio to the skillet, being careful not to overcrowd, and fry until they are golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes on each side.
Remove the cha gio from the oil with a slotted spoon and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil.
Serve the cha gio hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
